author: xrs_01
content: What do you guys think about using an execution tree to run multiple zkEVMs in parallel and recursively aggregate the proofs?

author: xrs_01
content: It lets you execute multiple blocks at once but with only logarithmic proving time

author: xrs_01
content: I’ve never heard of any other scaling solution with overhead logarithmic to the throughput

author: xrs_01
content: The caveat is that parallel executions can’t touch the same state —unless you handle those edge cases higher in the tree.
